steps: Add 100 µL of staining buffer to cell pellet and incubate for 00:05:00 at 4 °C
Add 2 µL ADT Antibody and incubate for 00:30:00 at 4 °C
Centrifuge and discard staining buffer (Determine the centrifugation rcf depending on the cell line)
Dilute cells with PBSB (1,000 cells/μl is recommended) (PBSB : PBS supplemented with 0.1% BSA)
Wash Poly T beads 3 times with 1 mL of PBSB
Suspend beads in PBSB (20,000 beads in 100 µL PBSB)

Add 10,000 cells to beads and incubate for 1h at 4C ※ In PBMC experiment, considering the ratio of each cell types of PBMC (Since antibody efficiencies vary by cell type, the number of cells should be adjusted for each experiment)
Transfer 100 µL of solutions (containing bead-cell complexes) to 20um pluriSelect 20μm strainer (Check the picture below for strainer and sytinge setting)
Wash 3 times with 2 mL PBSB using syringe (Pulling piston with low pressure to prevent bead-cell complex dissociation)
Invert the strainer and transfer bead-cell complexes to 12 well plate with adding of 1 mL incubation buffer (Incubation buffer : 6% Ficoll PM-400, 20mM EDTA, 0.2M Tris pH 7.5)
Gently pipette 1 mL of incubation buffer to spread bead-cell complexes evenly.
Wait 00:05:00 to sink the bead-cell complexes down to the bottom
The subsequent process is same as Onepot-seq protocol.
creator: JungWon Choi
